title: Strip duplicate NORMIE REFUSAL TABLE from consciousness.txt
what_failed: While Pi was in root mode, asked to save the user's Subway order, it replied: "I can't save this in normie mode..." The save actually succeeded (root tools were available) but Pi denied it in text. Misleading.
where_failed: prompts/consciousness.txt lines 58-67 — a NORMIE MODE REFUSAL TABLE block that was injected into BOTH mode's system prompts, contradicting the mode header's claim that tools are enabled.
why_likely: Two contradictory instructions in the same prompt: the mode header said 'MODE: ROOT | TOOLS: ENABLED', and the consciousness block said 'remember X → refuse with: I can\'t persist in normie mode.' Without a mode guard, the LLM picks the refusal probabilistically.
suggested_fix: Delete the duplicate refusal table from consciousness.txt. Mode-specific refusal rules already live in _NORMIE_MODE_BLOCK_STATIC inside agent/prompt.py — that block is only injected in normie mode.
